message:
chore: commit concurrent agent work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -80,7 +80,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1sp","title":"Epic: Alien CS Graveyard Integration for FrankenLibC","description":"Integration of highest-impact alien CS concepts: Separation Logic proofs for TSM (section 5.8), HTM fast-path for hot libc functions (section 4.2), Liquid Types for compile-time bounds checking (section 5.5), CHERI capability integration (section 3.7), RCU/QSBR for hot metadata (section 14.8), Flat Combining for contended state (section 14.2), Seqlocks for config hot paths (section 14.9), EBR for lock-free structures (section 14.10), PCC at FFI boundary (section 11.9), Property-based testing with shrinking (section 6.12).

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:59.056702175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:26.401338288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","cross-cutting","epic","frankenlibc","graveyard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sp","depends_on_id":"bd-2x5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1sp","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":694,"issue_id":"bd-1sp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sp.1","title":"Alien CS: RCU/QSBR for thread-local hot metadata (section 14.8)","description":"Implement RCU (Read-Copy-Update) with QSBR (Quiescent-State-Based Reclamation) for hot thread-local metadata reads. Zero-overhead reads for thread-local state. Covers TLS cache, arena metadata, and configuration hot paths. Must integrate with existing TSM validation pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"Validation commands:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane rcu (32 unit tests)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test rcu_qsbr_integration_test (16 integration tests)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --test rcu_qsbr_integration_test -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:47.330480659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:11:58.195905536Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","concurrency","frankenlibc","rcu"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sp.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1sp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1286,"issue_id":"bd-1sp.1","author":"RusticWolf","text":"Implemented RCU/QSBR module (rcu.rs + integration tests). 48 tests passing (32 unit + 16 integration). Workspace check/clippy clean. See bead notes for full details.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:11:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sp.10","title":"Alien CS: E2E tests - multi-concept interaction under realistic workloads","description":"E2E tests: verify all alien CS concepts compose correctly under realistic workloads. RCU + EBR interaction, flat combining + seqlock interaction, HTM fast-path + software fallback transition. Must test under thread counts from 1 to 32+ with mixed read/write ratios. Benchmark composite overhead vs individual concept overhead.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

-{"id":"bd-1sp.11","title":"Alien CS: Logging - per-concept evaluation traces, contention metrics","description":"Logging spec for alien CS concepts: TRACE for per-operation RCU/seqlock/EBR/flat-combining internals, DEBUG for contention metrics and epoch advancement, INFO for concept activation/deactivation and HTM fallback events, WARN for excessive contention or EBR grace period delays, ERROR for reclamation invariant violations. All records include trace_id+decision_id.

 {"id":"bd-1sp.2","title":"Alien CS: Flat Combining for contended libc state (section 14.2)","description":"Implement Flat Combining to convert contention into sequential batching on hot shared libc state. Target: malloc metadata, stdio FILE* locks, pthread mutex operations. Must demonstrate measurable scaling improvement under >4 thread contention.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1sp.3","title":"Alien CS: Seqlocks for configuration hot paths (section 14.9)","description":"Implement seqlocks for optimistic versioned reads on rarely-changing configuration data (locale settings, environment variables, TSM policy tables). ## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1sp.4","title":"Alien CS: EBR for lock-free data structures (section 14.10)","description":"Implement Epoch-Based Reclamation for safe memory reclamation in lock-free data structures without GC.
